text: You have a personal code of ethics to which you adhere. The specifics of this code must be worked out with the Director prior to play, and the character must follow it strictly.
mechanics: Characters with this Merit gain two additional dice to all Willpower or Virtue rolls when acting in accordance with their code (e.g., defending the helpless) or when attempting to avoid situations that might force them to violate their code.
title: Code Of Honor

text:
While some might refer to you as a 'cold fish,' you have a knack for separating factual reporting from emotional or hysterical coloration.
You may or may not be emotional yourself, but you can see clearly when others are clouding the facts with their feelings.
mechanics: -1 difficulty in interpreting information wherein emotionality would interfere with accuracy.
title: Coldly Logical

text: You have the ability to focus your mind and shut out any distractions or annoyances.
mechanics: You are unaffected by any penalties stemming from distracting circumstances (e.g., loud noises, strobe lights, or hanging upside down).
title: Concentration

text:
You have keen insight into the ulterior motives of all your actions.
Through this nightly exercise, you also have incredible insight into the underlying motives of others' actions.
mechanics: +2 dice to Perception rolls against a PC or NPC with the same Nature or Demeanor as you.
title: Introspection

text: You know a language in addition to your native one.
mechanics:
You can take this Merit multiple times, each reflecting a different language. 
This merit can be taken as many times as you want and does not count towards your merit cap.
title: Language

restrictions:
Sphere: Vampire
Cannot take merit 'Light Sleeper' & flaw 'Deep Sleeper'.
text: You can awaken instantly at any sign of trouble or danger, and do so without any sleepiness or hesitation.
mechanics: You may ignore rules regarding how Humanity restricts the number of dice available during the day.
title: Light Sleeper

text: You have a flair for languages.
mechanics: You may add three dice to any dice pool involving written or spoken languages, and each purchase of the Language Merit gives you two languages instead of just one.
title: Natural Linguist

text: You learn quickly.
mechanics: While the blood cost remains the same, the number of successful rolls needed to raise Talents, Skills or Knowledges (user choice) is cut in half.
HR: This merit has been modified from the original version to better suit this game.
title: Precocious

text: You have an innate sense of time and are able to estimate the passage of time accurately without using a watch or other mechanical device.
mechanics: You always know what time it is. I'd make fun of you, but sometimes you really need to know when that sun will rise or set, and you're on top of that. Kudos to you.
title: Time Sense

text: You have expertise in a specific field that makes your conversation intriguing to an older Kindred. So long as your knowledge holds the other Vampire's attention, they have a vested interest in keeping you around. Then again, once they've pumped you for every iota of information you possess, that patronage may suddenly vanish.
mechanics: This merit imparts a 1-dot Mentor with a specific interest. Unlike a Mentor, Useful Knowledge does not imply a permanent relationship.
title: Useful Knowledge

restrictions:
Cannot take flaw 'Hard Of Hearing' & merit 'Acute Senses: Hearing'.
Cannot take merit 'Acute Senses: Hearing' & flaw 'Deaf'.
Cannot take flaw 'Bad Sight' & merit 'Acute Senses: Sight'.
text: One of your senses is exceptionally sharp--sight, hearing, smell, touch, or taste.
mechanics: The difficulty for all tasks involving the use of this particular sense is reduced by two.
title: Acute Senses

text: You have a high degree of off-hand dexterity and can perform tasks with the 'wrong' hand at no penalty.
mechanics: The rules for taking multiple actions still apply, but you do not suffer a difficulty penalty if you use two weapons or are forced to use your off hand.
title: Ambidextrous

text:
You look more hale and healthy in appearance than other vampires, allowing you to blend with human society much more easily.
You still retain the color of a living mortal, and your skin feels only slightly cool to the touch.
mechanics: Increases difficulty by 2 on rolls to determine vampirism by non-vampires. (House Rule)
title: Blush Of Health

text:
Your appearance is sufficiently thug-like to inspire fear (or at least disquiet) in those who see you.
While you're not necessarily ugly, you do radiate a quiet menace, to the point where people cross the street to avoid passing near you.
mechanics: All Intimidation rolls against those who have not demonstrated their physical superiority to you are at -1 difficulty.
title: Bruiser

text: You possess an innately perfect sense of balance.
mechanics: Characters with this Merit reduce difficulties of all balance-related rolls (e.g., Dexterity + Athletics to walk along a narrow ledge) by two.
title: Catlike Balance

text: You are good at taking risks, and even better at surviving them.
mechanics:
+3 dice to rolls attempting exceptionally risky non-combat actions and negate a single botch die.
This action must be at least difficulty 8 and have the potential to inflict at least 3 health levels of damage.
title: Daredevil

restrictions: Sphere: Vampire. Cannot take flaw 'Deep Sleeper'.
text:
No one can explain it, but you seem to have the ability to work on less rest than your fellow vampires.
You always seem to be the first to rise and the last to go to bed even if you've been out until dawn.
mechanics: Your Humanitas is considered to be 10 for purposes of deciding when you rise each evening.
title: Early Riser

text:
You have the capacity to eat food and even savor its taste.
While you cannot derive any nourishment from eating regular foods, this ability will serve you well in pretending to be human.
Of course, you can't digest what you eat, and there will be some point during the evening when you have to heave it back up.
mechanics: This character does not immediately throw up food or drink upon consumption.
title: Eat Food

text: You are able to draw more than the usual amount of nourishment from blood.
mechanics:
Gain an additional point to your blood pool for every two points of blood you consume.
This does not allow you to exceed your blood pool maximum.
This does apply to human blood and vitae. (House Rule)
title: Efficient Digestion

restrictions:
null; See Rules: Social Rolls
Cannot take merit 'Enchanting Voice' & flaw 'Mute'.
text:
There is something about your voice that others cannot ignore.
When you command, they are cowed.
When you seduce, they swoon.
Whether thundering, soothing, persuading, or simply talking, your voice commands attention.
mechanics:
-2 difficulty to all rolls involving voice to persuade, charm, or command.
This bonus does not apply to the use of supernatural powers (ie: Dominate).
title: Enchanting Voice

restrictions:
Clan Nosferatu.
Cannot take merit 'Friendly Face' & flaw 'Disfigured'.
Cannot take merit 'Friendly Face' & flaw 'Monstrous'.
text:
You have a face that reminds everyone of someone, to the point where strangers are inclined to be well-inclined toward you because of it.
The effect doesn't fade even if you explain the 'mistake'.
mechanics:
-1 difficulty on all appropriate Social-based rolls (yes for Seduction, no for Intimidation, for example) when a stranger is involved.
This Merit only functions on a first meeting.
title: Friendly Face

text: You are abnormally large in size, at least 6'10" and 300 pounds in weight.
mechanics:
Gain an additional Bruised health level.
+2 dice to push objects (non-combat), open barred doors, and avoid being knocked down. (House Rule)
This merit is identical to 'Sturdy', but has character height/weight requirements.
title: Huge Size

text:
You were once blood-bound but have secretly slipped the leash,
and you are free to act as you will once more.
Your regnant has no idea that you are not in fact bound,
and continues to treat you as if you were.
mechanics: Your character should be blood bound, but is not.
title: Broken Bond

text:
You're part of the brute squad the local kindred law enforcement calls on when they need some muscle.
As a result, you get in on action that others miss entirely, score points with those in power, and occasionally get a chance to act outside of the law. 
How far outside the law you can go depends on circumstance and how much the Vampire you report to likes you.
mechanics:
+2 opinion of all Camarilla law enforcement personnel.
Your character will be called into combat operations on a routine basis without boon payment.
title: Bullyboy

text:
One particular Clan (not your own) has a special liking for you.
You might have done the Clan as a whole a favor at some point,
or perhaps you're just a loud voice in support of their aims.
mechanics:
-2 diff on all friendly Social rolls involving members of that Clan.
+1 - +4 opinion of NPC members of that clan, decided randomly.
title: Clan Friendship

text:
You spend an unusual amount of time in Elysium.
You see and are seen to such an extent that all of the movers and shakers of Elysium at least know who you are.
Extended time spent in Elysium also gives you extended opportunities to interact with the Harpies and other Kindred of that stature,
and they'll know your name when you approach them.
mechanics:
This merit essentially equates to 'Contact 1: Harpies'.
It also confers +1 opinion of all NPC harpies to your character.
HR: This merit has been changed to better accommodate gameplay.
title: Elysium Regular

text:
You were introduced to the Blood long before you were made Kindred.
Your long experience as a ghoul gives you insight into and comfort with vampiric society.
mechanics:
-1 diff on all Social rolls when used on other neonates.
-1 diff on all rolls relating to vampiric knowledge.
title: Former Ghoul

text:
While you're not a Nosferatu, you know your way around the sewers, tunnels, ducts, subway tubes, and other subterranean passages of your hometown.
The local Nosferatu (and any other creatures dwelling down in the muck) may not actually like you, but they're not inclined to kill you on sight when they see you in their territory.
mechanics:
-1 diff on any rolls involving navigating the underground world.
+3 opinion of all NPC Nosferatu.
title: Friend Of The Underground

text:
Everyone in the city knows you, and knows that you're no threat to their plans.
While that sort of estimation may seem insulting, it's also what's kept you from being killed.
No one considers you worth their time to deal with, and that low opinion keeps you safe.
mechanics:
There's no mechanical benefit, but, there's a lot of benefit in avoiding negative NPC interaction.
If you start acting in a way that demonstrates that you are no longer harmless, others' reactions to you will
likely change as a result.
title: Harmless

text:
For whatever reason (maybe your winning smile or perhaps just your superb groveling technique), the local Sheriff in charge of discipline likes you.
He's inclined to overlook your minor trespasses and let you in on things you're not supposed to know about.
He even gives you warnings about occasional crackdowns and times when the higher-ups aren't feeling generous.
mechanics:
Taking this merit gives a special relation with the Sheriff of the Praxis.
Abusing this connection might well turn a friendly Vampire into an enemy -- and the
change might not be apparent until it's too late.
title: Lawman's Friend

text:
You have an informer buried in one of your Sect's enemy organizations who funnels you all sorts of information
as to what her peers are up to.
What you do with the information is up to you, but abusing the knowledge might be a good way to get your
informer killed.
mechanics: This merit confers an 'ally' (background) at level 3 in an enemy faction.
title: Mole

text: You are gifted with a certain magnetism to which others naturally defer.
mechanics:
+2 dice to all leadership rolls.
A Charisma of 3 or greater is required to take this merit.
title: Natural Leader

text:
An acquaintance from your breathing days was Embraced at the same time you were.
Your friendship has endured even death and unlife, and you find a constant source of support and aid in your old friend.
They expect the same of you, which isn't always convenient, but at least you each have someone to hang onto who remembers the good old nights -- and days.
mechanics:
Old Pal counts as a level 2 ally who's loyalty you can pretty much always count on.
They will call on you to use your action, on occasion, to help them; you can do the same from them.
title: Old Pal

text:
Unlike many Kindred, you like to travel.
You have a solid knowledge of safe travel routes and methodologies, not to mention haven space available in any number of destinations.
Unless someone out there knows your exact route and is specifically looking for you, you can move between cities
unimpeded by random encounters with Lupines, overzealous state troopers, and the like.
mechanics: You (and the groups you lead) can travel distances without being impeded, except in the aforementioned conditions.
title: Open Road

text:
Your sire has or had great status in her Sect or Clan, and this has accorded you a certain amount of prestige.
Though your sire may no longer have any dealings with you, the simple fact of your ancestry has marked you forever.
This prestige might aid you greatly in dealings with other vampires, or it might engender jealousy or contempt.
mechanics: Potentially +1 or -1 to opinions of key vampires, on a case by case basis.
title: Prestigious Sire

text:
Your sire watched you for some time before Embracing you, and spoke glowingly of you to acquaintances.
These vampires may be inclined to look favorably on you by dint of your sire's recommendation.
mechanics: -1 diff on Social rolls with all who have heard good things about you.
title: Protege

text:
You're one of the up-and-comers in the city, a rising star in your Sect.
Everyone wants to know you and be your friend, even as those in power groom you for positions of greater responsibility.
mechanics:
+1 opinion and -1 diff to all social rolls against any vampires in your Sect who aren't actively opposing
your ascent.
title: Rising Star

text:
This Merit is sometimes called the halo effect.
Everyone considers you pure and innocent, though not necessarily naive.
You have a saint-like quality that is hard to pinpoint but cannot be denied.
You are trusted, even if you are not trustworthy.
mechanics:
+1 opinion of the majority of kindred in the city.
You tend to receive lesser punishments for wrongdoing.
title: Sanctity

text:
You have taken the time to learn about and specialize in a supernatural faction that is not necessarily your enemy.
You are aware of at least some of the group's customs, strategies, abilities, and long-term goals, and can put that knowledge to good use.
mechanics:
-2 diff for all non-combat rolls pertaining to the subject of specialization.
+1 diff for all non-combat rolls pertaining to other enemies, because you're so focused on your field.
HR:
While you do not get -2 diff for combat actions, you do get -2 diff for creating and implementing strategy
against the chosen group.
You cannot take the merit 'Scholar of Enemies' and the merit 'Scholar of Others'.
title: Scholar Of Others

text:
You can take one additional Discipline as if it were
a clan discipline. All costs to learn that discipline
are paid out as if it were native to your clan.
This merit can only be taken once.
Caitiff cannot take this merit.
mechanics: As stated above.
HR:
As a Vampire can only naturally have three disciplines
on this game, this merit is exceptionally useful.
The only other way to get disciplines is through
diablerie.
Only common disciplines can be taken with this merit.
title: Additional Discipline

text: Your aura is unnaturally bright and colorful for a Vampire.
mechanics: You register as a mortal on all attempts to read your aura.
title: Deceptive Aura

text:
Normally vampires can only seal the wounds they
inflict from feeding by licking them. With but a touch,
you can achieve the same effect, closing the puncture
wounds left by drinking blood.
mechanics: You can seal puncture marks by lick or by touch.
title: Healing Touch

text:
The tell-tale black streaks of diablerie do not
manifest in your aura.
mechanics: Diablerie cannot be told by looking at your aura.
title: Hidden Diablerie

text:
With rare exceptions, animals usually despise the
Kindred. Some flee, others attack, but all dislike being
in the presence of a Vampire. You have no such problem.
Animals may not enjoy being in your company, but they
don't actively flee from you.
mechanics: Animals will not automatically run from or attack you.
HR:
In a world where horses are the only reliable & fast method
of transportation, this merit is far more useful now.
title: Inoffensive To Animals

text:
You were born lucky -- or else the Devil looks after
his own.
mechanics:
You can repeat any one (and only one) roll, including
botches, in a scene. The re-roll must be announced
immediately after failure.
title: Lucky

text:
You possess the natural affinity to sense and hear spirits
ghosts, and shades. Though you cannot see them, you can
sense them, speak to them and, through pleading or 
cajoling, draw them to your presence. You may call upon
them for aid or advice, but there will always be a price.
mechanics:
You can speak and deal with spirits.
-2 diff for Awareness rolls involving spirits of the dead.
title: Medium

text:
Fate has granted you the opportunity to come as
close to Final Death as anyone can get and still survive.
mechanics:
When a roll occurs that would result in your death, the
roll is made again. If the next roll succeeds, then you
live -- and one of your nine lives is used up.
If that subsequent roll fails, then another reroll is made,
until either a successful roll occurs or your nine lives are
used up.
title: Nine Lives

text:
You can see and interpret signs and omens. You are
able to draw advice from these omens, for they provide
hints of the future and warnings of the present.
mechanics:
When Staff feels that you are in position to see an omen
a Perception+Awareness roll will be made with the difficulty
relative to how well the omen is concealed.
If successful, you may then roll Intelligence + Occult to
interpret what you have seen; the difficulty is again
relative to the complexity of the omen.
title: Oracular Ability

text:
You have a ghostly companion and guide.
The identity and exact powers of this spirit are up to Staff.
but it can be called upon in difficult situations for help and guidance
mechanics: The spirit acts as a mentor of Mentor 3.
HR:
There is a serious advantage of taking this merit:
It is extremely hard for most clans to kill a spirit.
title: Spirit Mentor

text:
You have discovered, perhaps too late, a true love.
They are mortal, but are the center of your existence,
and inspire you to keep going in a world of darkness
and despair. Whenever you suffer, the thought of your
true love gives you the strength to persevere.
mechanics:
This merit grants you one automatic success on
Willpower rolls, which can be negated only by
a botched die. This can be a great gift but also
a hindrance, for your true love may require
protection and occasionally rescue.
title: true Love

text: You are immune to being blood bound.
mechanics: As stated above.
title: Unbondable
